channel.title: Indian Defence Research Wing
channel.description: INDIAN DEFENCE NEWS, LATEST DEFENCE NEWS, INDIAN ARMY, INDIAN AIR FORCE, INDIAN NAVY, DEFENSE NEWS

item.title: India Plans S4-Class SSBN Conversion into SSGN to Boost Long-Range Strike Power Before SSNs Arrive
item.description: India’s nuclear submarine roadmap may be entering a pragmatic and strategically significant phase, as naval planners evaluate the possibility of converting the S4 SSBN platform into a nuclear-powered guided-missile submarine (SSGN). According to sources, the idea is gaining traction within design circles as a cost-effective and time-efficient way to plug a critical capability gap while India’s indigenous SSN program under Project-77 remains in its early stages.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: DRDO Begins Development of GaN-Based AESA Radar for Netra Mk2 AEW&C With Near-300° Surveillance
item.description: India’s upcoming Netra Mk-2 AEW&C program represents a major technological leap over the current Netra airborne early warning system. Designed to eliminate the limitations of earlier platforms and introduce next-generation radar technologies, the aircraft will dramatically improve the Indian Air Force’s long-range surveillance, tracking, and battle-management capabilities.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: AMCA Propulsion Roadmap Highlights GTRE–Safran Consortium Strategy
item.description: India’s next leap in combat aviation propulsion may be shaped by a consortium-style industrial model, as the Gas Turbine Research Establishment (GTRE) explores a broad partnership framework for developing the 120kN-class engine intended to power the Advanced Medium Combat Aircraft programme. According to industry discussions, the proposed approach — likely involving collaboration with Safran — is designed to ensure that the engine is ultimately manufactured entirely within India, supported by a deep domestic supplier ecosystem rather than reliance on external component streams.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: l-114-300 Nears Certification After Arctic Trials; India Deal in Focus
item.description: Russia’s Ilyushin Il-114-300 regional turboprop has officially completed its flight test cycle, marking a major milestone in its development as an “import-substituted” civilian aircraft. The completion of trials concludes an extensive evaluation phase aimed at validating the aircraft’s reliability, performance, and compliance with certification standards.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India Draws Lessons from West Asia War, Pushes for Anti-Drone Shield and Decentralised Warfare Doctrine
item.description: Drawing critical lessons from the ongoing conflict in West Asia, India’s military leadership has initiated high-level discussions on strengthening counter-drone capabilities and restructuring warfighting doctrines to address emerging threats. Senior military officers indicate that the focus is now shifting toward the large-scale procurement of anti-drone systems and their integration with legacy air defence platforms to build a layered and cost-effective defensive umbrella against swarm drone attacks.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Pakistan Must Upgrade Signals and Human Intelligence to Thwart Indian Decapitation Strike Risks: Former Envoy
item.description: In a candid assessment of future Pakistan-India conflicts, Dr Imran Ali, Pakistan’s former Ambassador to Oman, has called on the country’s military leadership to dramatically increase investment in electronic signals intelligence (SIGINT) and human intelligence (HUMINT) networks. His remarks come amid heightened concerns over sophisticated decapitation strikes that could target top commanders and political figures in multi-domain warfare scenarios.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: From Here’s Your Pilot and Tea” to “Legs Shaking and Sweat on Forehead”: The Stark Contrast in How Nations Handle Pilot Recoveries and High-Stakes Crises
item.description: The online memes comparing global military operations have taken a sharper edge with fresh revelations from Pakistan’s own political circles about the 2019 release of Indian Air Force Wing Commander Abhinandan Varthaman. While the United States focuses on mission success and pilot safety even at the cost of expensive aircraft, and India stresses precision strikes with all personnel returning safely, Pakistan’s narrative of courteous hospitality now stands juxtaposed against internal accounts of panic at the highest levels.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: BrahMos Missile Developer Alexander Leonov Dies at 74: A Major Loss to Indo-Russian Defense Collaboration
item.description: A top Russian missile designer, Alexander Leonov, linked to the development of hypersonic missile Zircon and new generation Indo-Russian Brahmos NG, died at the age of 74 on Sunday, local media reported.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: US intelligence warns Iran unlikely to ease Strait of Hormuz chokehold soon
item.description: Recent US intelligence reports warn that Iran is unlikely to open the Strait of Hormuz any time soon because its grip on the world’s most vital oil artery provides the only real leverage it has over the United States, according to three sources familiar with the matter.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Families of AI171 crash victims seek release of black box data, write to PM
item.description: Ten months after the tragic Air India plane crash that killed 260 persons, bereaved families of the victims have written to Prime Minister Narendra Modi, urging the release of the Cockpit Voice Recorder (CVR) and black box data.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India’s Role Seen As Vital To Bolster Bangladesh-Nepal Economic Ties
item.description: Nepal’s first billionaire, Binod K Chaudhary, has said that Bangladesh and Nepal could strengthen economic ties in energy and cross-border trade, with closer regional cooperation involving India. “We would like to enter into a much bigger economic engagement with Bangladesh, but without India playing a positive role, that’s not going to happen,” Chaudhary said on Saturday at a press conference organised by the International Chamber of Commerce Bangladesh (ICCB) in Dhaka, according to a report in The Daily Star.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Pak-Linked Networks Using AI, Coordinated Amplification To Influence Bangladesh’s Digital Discourse: Report
item.description: Pakistan-linked disinformation networks are actively targeting Bangladesh through coordinated digital campaigns, leveraging social media platforms, artificial intelligence tools and local amplification channels to shape public narratives and geopolitical perceptions, according to a recent investigation.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Red Corridor Is History: Nation Closes A Half-Century Wound; The Story Of How And Who
item.description: Picture the forests of Bastar in 2013. No roads. No mobile signals. No banks. No schools worth the name. A parallel government, complete with its own courts, its own tax collectors and its own executioners, ruled these jungles by absolute terror. Tribal families buried their dead in silence, fearing that grief itself might draw suspicion. Security forces moved in convoys because moving alone meant dying.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: All Indian Seafarers In Persian Gulf Are Safe: Govt
item.description: All Indian seafarers in the Persian Gulf region are safe, and no incident involving Indian-flagged vessels has been reported in the past 24 hours, according to a statement issued by the Ministry of Ports, Shipping and Waterways on Sunday. A total of 17 Indian-flagged vessels with 460 Indian seafarers remain in the western Persian Gulf region, with Director General (DG) Shipping, in coordination with ship owners, placing agencies and Indian Missions, actively monitoring the situation, the statement said.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: EAM Jaishankar Speaks To Iranian Foreign Minister Amid Escalating Regional Tensions
item.description: External Affairs Minister (EAM) S. Jaishankar on Sunday said he received a call from his Iranian counterpart, Abbas Araghchi, during which the two leaders discussed the prevailing situation in the region.
item.dc:creator: admin
